title: Runebound Retrospective - Episode 4 - The Sands of Al-Kalim
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=W3I8gwLv0Ro

The Sands of Al-Kalim brings Runebound's exploration to a legendary desert realm—a place of mirages, ancient magic, and the freedom to chart your own destiny. Daniel acknowledges this expansion as his least favorite setting thematically, yet it showcases innovations that enrich solo play significantly. Instead of racing to defeat a single foe, you'll pursue four legendary quests: recruiting allies, taming mighty mounts, acquiring items, or crafting runes.

The expansion introduces clever mechanics like day/night cycles that alter enemy difficulty, a roaming storm system that reshapes the map, and three legendary cities offering unique bazaar effects. Survival elements—managing thirst and water—echo the Frozen Wastes' approach but with inverted pressure. What makes Al-Kalim shine is its press-your-luck element: bonus encounters can strip away hard-earned quests, forcing meaningful decision-making. Some solo players may find that frustrating, but Daniel appreciates how it creates narrative tension and forces pivoting strategies.

----

Which Runebound expansion's quest structure—the journey toward the goal—matters more to you than the destination itself?
